steps.title: Find what the narrator actually does walking across the stage
steps.content: The passage states: "I did what my teacher had advised. Pretend you're only playing for one person. That way you won't be so nervous." Then the narrator glances at her father and closes her eyes. The narrator is explicitly following her teacher's advice to manage nervousness.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'She thinks about the differences between playing the piano alone and playing in a band.': The narrator thinks about her teacher's advice, not about solo versus band playing. ✗
  • Choice B — 'She feels excitement about playing in a music festival for the first time.': The text says she is nervous, not excited, and nothing indicates this is her first festival. ✗
  • Choice C — 'She remembers how hard she has practiced for the performance.': No mention of practice appears; she is recalling her teacher's anti-nervousness strategy. ✗
  • Choice D — 'She follows her teacher's advice about managing her nervousness.': Matches exactly — she does what her teacher advised to avoid being nervous. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
